label: Localized bee sting without infection or anaphylaxis
summary: Your symptoms fit a local skin reaction from a bee sting.
found: Your clinician diagnosed a localized bee sting. We did not find signs of anaphylaxis, serious allergic reaction, spreading infection, or a sting emergency today.
home: Wash the area gently with soap and water.
Use cool compresses for itching or swelling.
meds: Use itch or allergy medicines only if prescribed or recommended.
Use acetaminophen or ibuprofen only if you can take it safely and follow the label.
return: Trouble breathing, throat swelling, vomiting after a sting, fainting, or a bodywide reaction.
Spreading redness, pus, fever, red streaks, or worsening warmth.
Symptoms that worsen or do not improve as expected.
follow: Follow up with primary care, urgent care, or the ED if symptoms are not improving or your clinician told you to recheck.
inclusion: Adult with clinician diagnosis or discharge impression of localized bee sting.
No anaphylaxis, systemic allergic reaction, secondary infection, necrotic spider or scorpion concern, tick-borne illness concern, or snakebite concern.
exclusion: Anaphylaxis, systemic allergic reaction, secondary infection, necrotic spider or scorpion concern, tick-borne illness concern, snakebite concern, immunocompromised host, or specialist-directed allergy plan.
source_audit_notes: MedlinePlus insect bite and sting material supports local bite/sting care, itch control, and escalation for allergic reactions, infection, or systemic symptoms. This expansion is limited to clinician-diagnosed localized bee sting with anaphylaxis and infection red flags absent.
required_modifiers: adult with clinician diagnosis of localized bee sting
